lede: A look at April 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kurt Georg Kiesinger and Black Francis, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Humboldt Broncos
events.desc: A bus carrying the Humboldt Broncos junior ice hockey team collides with a semi-truck in Saskatchewan, Canada, killing 16 people and injuring 13 others.

events.title: 2009 L'Aquila earthquake
events.desc: A 6.3 magnitude earthquake strikes near L'Aquila, Italy, killing 307.

events.title: Rolandas Paksas
events.desc: Rolandas Paksas becomes the first president of Lithuania to be peacefully removed from office by impeachment.

events.title: Greece
events.desc: The flag carrier airline of Greece for decades, Olympic Airways, is founded by Aristotle Onassis following the acquisition of "TAE - Greek National Airlines".

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Nazi Germany launches Operation 25 (the invasion of Kingdom of Yugoslavia) and Operation Marita (the invasion of Greece).

events.title: Varney Air Lines
events.desc: Varney Airlines makes its first commercial flight (Varney is the root company of United Airlines).

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: The United States declares war on Germany.

events.title: John Jacob Astor
events.desc: John Jacob Astor incorporates the American Fur Company, that would eventually make him America's first millionaire.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: Ships of the Continental Navy fail in their attempt to capture a Royal Navy dispatch boat.

events.title: New York Slave Revolt of 1712
events.desc: The New York Slave Revolt of 1712 begins near Broadway.

births.name: Kurt Georg Kiesinger
births.line: German lawyer, politician and Chancellor of Germany

births.name: Rafael Correa
births.line: Ecuadorian economist and politician, 54th President of Ecuador

births.name: Feodor Lynen
births.line: German biochemist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Anita Borg, American computer scientist and educator; founded Anita Borg Institute for Women and Technology
Juvénal Habyarimana, Rwandan banker and politician, 3rd President of Rwanda
Igor Stravinsky, Russian-American pianist, composer, and conductor
Albrecht Dürer, German painter, engraver, and mathematician

observances.text: Chakri Day, commemorating the establishment of the Chakri dynasty. (Thailand)
Christian feast day: Albrecht Dürer and Lucas Cranach (Lutheran Church).
